name: rownd-django

classes.name: rownd_django.auth.jwks_client.PyJWKClient
documentation: Pulled from PyJWT and modified for our purposes

modules.name: rownd_django.settings
documentation:
Settings for Rownd are all namespaced in the ROWND setting.
For example your project's `settings.py` file might look like this:
ROWND = {
    'APP_KEY': '<your app key>',
    'APP_SECRET': '<your app secret>',
}
This module provides the `rownd_setting` object, that is used to access
Rownd settings, checking for user settings first, then falling
back to the defaults.

functions.name: rownd_django.settings.perform_import
documentation:
If the given setting is a string import notation,
then perform the necessary import or imports.

functions.name: rownd_django.settings.import_from_string
documentation: Attempt to import a class from a string representation.

classes.name: rownd_django.settings.RowndSettings
documentation:
A settings object that allows Rownd settings to be accessed as
properties. For example:
    from rownd.settings import rownd_settings
    print(rownd_settings.API_URL)
Any setting with string import paths will be automatically resolved
and return the class, rather than the string literal.
Note:
This is an internal class that is only compatible with settings namespaced
under the ROWND name. It is not intended to be used by 3rd-party
apps, and test helpers like `override_settings` may not work as expected.
